name: d3-data-visualizer
description: Asks the user what data to analyse, designs the optimal visualization approach, then generates a self-contained HTML file using D3.js from CDN with embedded HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.

gather-requirements.do: Use the AskUserQuestion tool to ask the user what data they want to visualize. Ask them to describe: (1) the data source or dataset they want to analyse, (2) what kind of insights or story they want the visualization to tell, and (3) any preferences for chart type (bar, line, scatter, pie, treemap, force-directed graph, etc.) or if they want you to recommend the best approach. Collect their response and summarize the requirements clearly.
gather-requirements.produces: A clear summary of the user's data, goals, and visualization preferences

design-visualization.do: Based on the user's requirements, design the optimal D3.js visualization approach. Consider: (1) the best chart type for the data and goals described, (2) the data structure needed (arrays, nested objects, etc.), (3) color scheme and layout decisions, (4) any interactivity (tooltips, hover effects, transitions, zoom, filtering), (5) responsive design considerations. If the user provided raw data, plan how to embed it. If they described a data pattern, plan how to generate representative sample data. Write a detailed visualization specification including chart type, axes, scales, color palette, dimensions, and interactive features.
design-visualization.produces: A detailed visualization design specification including chart type, data structure, scales, colors, interactivity plan, and layout

generate-html.do: Generate a single self-contained HTML file that implements the visualization design. The file MUST: (1) load D3.js from CDN (https://d3js.org/d3.v7.min.js) via a <script> tag, (2) embed ALL CSS in a <style> block in the <head>, (3) embed ALL JavaScript in a <script> block, (4) embed the data directly in JavaScript (no external data files), (5) include a clean, modern design with proper fonts (use Google Fonts or system fonts), (6) be fully responsive, (7) include tooltips or hover interactions where appropriate, (8) include a title and any necessary labels/legends, (9) use smooth transitions and animations for visual polish. Write the file to 'visualization.html' in the current working directory. Make sure the visualization is production-quality and visually appealing with proper margins, padding, and spacing.
generate-html.produces: A self-contained HTML file at ./visualization.html with embedded D3.js visualization

summary.do: Provide a concise summary to the user of what was created. Include: (1) the chart type and why it was chosen, (2) key features of the visualization (interactivity, responsiveness, etc.), (3) the file path where the HTML was saved (./visualization.html), (4) instructions on how to open it (just open the HTML file in any modern browser — no server needed since D3.js loads from CDN). Mention that they can modify the embedded data array in the JavaScript to update the visualization with their own data if sample data was used.
summary.produces: A user-friendly summary of the generated visualization and how to use it
